analyses.summary: Analog Devices is the top setup of June 18 — the tightest VCP in chip equipment, room to run on RSI (56), explosive earnings growth, and TV unanimous at STRONG BUY. Apple-Intel partnership announced today is a direct pipeline catalyst.
analyses.full_analysis: ADI designs mixed-signal (analog/digital bridge) semiconductors used in industrial automation, healthcare, automotive, and communications — the unsexy backbone of every electronic system. The stock is sitting 1.4% below its all-time high in a 5-contraction VCP with tightest contraction at 3.1%. That level of tightening means institutional sellers have exhausted themselves and supply is thin — a modest volume surge can drive a clean breakout above $437. Today's Apple-Intel partnership announcement is not directly about ADI but does accelerate the entire semiconductor equipment and analog supply chain. RSI at 56 means this move is young. EPS grew +110% YoY on revenue of +37% — analog companies don't usually post numbers like this; it reflects the upcycle intensifying. TradingView shows 16 buy signals and 0 sells, which is as clean a technical confirmation as exists.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +110%, revenue +37% — exceptional for an analog chip company. Gross margins above 65%; ROE above 18%. Industrial and auto end markets are recovering simultaneously.
signals: Industrial + auto recovery both in play

technical.summary: VCP with 5 contractions (tightest 3.1%), RSI 56, RS line at new highs, 1.4% from all-time high. Textbook pre-breakout coiled spring. Volume is the only thing needed to confirm.

earnings.summary: No earnings in the immediate entry window — next report expected late August. Beat-and-raise trajectory intact. No binary event risk here.

analyst.summary: Broad analyst buy consensus with targets clustered $450-500. Citi and Morgan Stanley have reiterated Outperform on WFE cycle uplift.

valuation.summary: Trading at approximately 28x forward earnings — premium but earned given the margin profile and growth. Not cheap, not stretched.

insider.summary: No material insider buying or selling in the last 90 days. Absence of selling at the high is a mild positive.
signals: No insider selling at the high

risk.summary: R:R approximately 2.3:1 with $396 stop and $517 target. Primary risks are semi sector rotation and a broader market correction. Individual stock risk is low given the fundamental setup.

catalyst.summary: Apple-Intel partnership today accelerates the entire analog supply chain. WFE upgrade cycle is the multi-quarter tailwind. Industrial automation recovery is the multi-year tailwind.

analyses.summary: Texas Instruments is the cleanest analog semiconductor setup in the scan — RSI 54 with 35 days to earnings (low binary risk), tight VCP, RS at new highs. The industrial recovery story is well underway and TXN has the broadest customer base in analog.
analyses.full_analysis: TI is the world's largest analog chip company by revenue, selling into 100,000+ customers across industrial, automotive, personal electronics, and communications. Its business is deliberately boring — analog chips don't get media attention — but that's the advantage. The VCP has 5 contractions with tightest at 3.7%, and RSI is only 54 — very early in what could be a multi-week run. Earnings are July 23, giving 35 days of pre-report runway. EPS grew +31% and revenue +19%. On the chart, TI is attempting to reclaim the $325 prior high, which if it succeeds opens a clean path to $377+. TradingView has 14 buy signals and 3 sells — not the overwhelming confirmation of ADI but still solidly in Buy territory. The analog cycle typically runs 12-18 months and we appear to be ~6 months into this one, suggesting further upside.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +31%, revenue +19%. TI's analog moat — 100,000+ customers and 300+ factories — creates enormous switching-cost advantages. FCF generation is industry-leading.

technical.summary: VCP 5 contractions tightest 3.7%, RSI 54 (maximum runway), RS at new highs. Attempting reclaim of $325 resistance.

earnings.summary: Earnings July 23 — 35 days away, a clean pre-report drift window. Recent trajectory is beat-and-guide-up. No binary event risk for the next 30 days.
signals: Earnings July 23 — low near-term binary risk

analyst.summary: Citi is pounding the table on TI and the analog recovery. Consensus Buy with targets $340-380.

valuation.summary: ~23x forward earnings — reasonable for a franchise with TI's moat and capital return program.

risk.summary: R:R 2.5:1 ($289 stop, $377 target). Trim to half before July 23 earnings if you've had a run. Semi sector rotation remains the main risk.

catalyst.summary: Apple-Intel partnership today accelerates demand for analog ICs across the supply chain. Industrial automation recovery and automotive electrification are the multi-year drivers.

analyses.summary: ASML is the only company in the world that makes EUV lithography machines — without them, advanced semiconductors (3nm, 2nm) cannot be built. It's the most essential pick-and-shovel in AI infrastructure. VCP, RS new high, TV unanimous, earnings July 15.
analyses.full_analysis: ASML's EUV machines cost $150-200M each and take years to build. TSMC, Intel, and Samsung must all buy from ASML — there is no alternative. This is not a competitive-moat story; it is a monopoly. The stock sits at an all-time high with a 7-contraction VCP (tightest 5.0%) — the most mature consolidation in the chip equipment group, meaning accumulation has been deep and distribution hasn't happened. RSI is 68 — not overbought, confirming the breakout is technically sound. Revenue is growing because AI capex is growing: every new AI accelerator chip built by TSMC or Intel requires ASML's machines. Earnings are July 15 (27 days away), which is a prime pre-earnings drift window. History shows ASML tends to gap up on results when the AI capex narrative is strong. TV has 16 buy signals and 0 sells — unanimous.

fundamentals.summary: Revenue growing with backlog at record levels; EUV ASPs rising as demand exceeds supply. Net margins above 25%. Only supplier in the world for EUV machines.

technical.summary: VCP 7 contractions tightest 5.0% — most mature consolidation in the group. RS new high. RSI 68. At ATH. TV: STRONG BUY (16/0).

earnings.summary: Earnings July 15 — 27-day pre-earnings drift window. Prior beats have been consistent. Management guidance tends to be conservative, so beats are the norm.

analyst.summary: Unanimous Buy across major banks; targets $2,000-2,400. Morgan Stanley says AI capex revision cycle just beginning.

valuation.summary: ~40x forward PE — elevated but ASML always trades at a premium due to its monopoly position. Premium is justified; the question is whether it expands.

insider.summary: No notable insider sales near the all-time high — a positive signal at this price level.

risk.summary: FX headwind (EUR/USD); export control risk if US-China tensions escalate (China is 15% of ASML sales). R:R ~2.5:1 with $1,765 stop.

catalyst.summary: Every AI accelerator built anywhere in the world uses ASML's machines. Earnings July 15 is the immediate catalyst; Intel Arizona foundry ramp is the 12-month catalyst.
signals: Intel foundry ramp = new ASML demand

analyses.summary: TSMC is the picks-and-shovels of global AI — every leading AI chip (NVIDIA H100/B200, Apple M4, AMD MI300) is built at TSMC. ATH, RS new high, RSI 59, EPS +58%. Earnings July 16 create a 28-day pre-report drift window.
analyses.full_analysis: TSMC has manufacturing technology roughly 1-2 generations ahead of Intel and Samsung. When NVIDIA needs to build its next GPU or Apple needs an M-series chip, there is exactly one foundry in the world capable of fabricating it: TSMC. This structural advantage creates pricing power and backlog visibility. The stock is at an all-time high with RSI 59 (room to run) and EPS growing +58% on revenue of +35%. TradingView shows 15 buy signals. The 28-day pre-earnings window before July 16 is the window to be in the trade; the risk is a geopolitical headline (Taiwan Strait) which can cause sharp sudden drops — position sizing should reflect this tail risk. Users already holding at $175 avg cost are sitting on a 156% gain; this analysis covers new buys at $449.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +58%, revenue +35%. Advanced packaging leadership (CoWoS for AI GPUs). Customer lock-in is near-permanent — retooling for a different foundry takes years.
signals: CoWoS packaging monopoly for AI GPUs

technical.summary: ATH, RS new high, RSI 59 — room to run without being overbought. TV: BUY (15/1). Clean entry at current levels.

earnings.summary: Earnings July 16 — 28-day pre-report drift window. Consistent beat history; next report likely strong given AI GPU demand.

analyst.summary: Broad Buy consensus with targets $480-520. Morgan Stanley says 2026 AI chip demand will exceed capacity.

valuation.summary: ~22x forward PE — reasonable for a franchise with TSMC's moat and growth trajectory.

risk.summary: Geopolitical tail risk is real and non-zero. Position size at 70% of normal max to reflect Taiwan Strait risk. Trim 30% before July 16 earnings.

catalyst.summary: NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra, Apple M5, AMD MI400 all fabbed at TSMC in 2026. Earnings July 16. Intel partnership for 3nm production is a new long-term revenue stream.

analyses.summary: Micron is one of the best fundamental stories in the scan — AI memory (HBM) driving +756% EPS and +196% revenue — BUT earnings are in 7 days (June 25). Do NOT initiate a full position here. Trim existing to half before June 25; re-add post-earnings on strength.
analyses.full_analysis: Micron's HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) for AI accelerators has been the surprise growth driver of the current memory cycle. NVIDIA's H100 and B200 use Micron's HBM3E alongside SK Hynix, and supply is still tight. The result: EPS went from near-zero to +756% YoY and revenue surged +196%. The problem right now is the 7-day earnings countdown. Strong stocks often drift 5-10% higher into reports on momentum then drop sharply on 'sell the news' — Micron has done exactly this in prior cycles. The risk-adjusted trade is to trim existing positions to half before June 25 and reassess post-report. If MU holds $1,000 after reporting, it likely re-initiates a fresh leg higher — that's the re-entry point. New buyers should wait entirely.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +756%, revenue +196% — memory cycle supercycle numbers. HBM3E for AI GPUs is a new, high-ASP revenue stream that didn't exist two years ago. DRAM prices are firming.
signals: HBM3E for AI GPUs — new high-ASP stream

technical.summary: At 52-week high, TV BUY (15/1). But: RSI and price are at extreme levels — technically strong but overbought short-term going into a binary event.

earnings.summary: CRITICAL: Earnings June 25 — 7 days away. Binary event risk is the dominant factor. 'Sell the news' is common even after strong beats. Trim to half before June 25.

analyst.summary: Broadly bullish with targets $1,100-1,400. Barron's recently featured MU on Apple-memory demand.

valuation.summary: Memory valuations are cyclical — current PE looks cheap on this cycle's peak, expensive if memory softens. Price in the cycle appropriately.

risk.summary: 7 days to earnings is the single biggest risk. Trim to half NOW. Stop is $1,019. If you hold through earnings and it gaps down, your stop will be blown through — plan accordingly.

catalyst.summary: HBM3E supply ramp for NVIDIA B200 and Apple M5. DRAM pricing inflection. Earnings June 25 is the near-term catalyst (or risk).

analyses.summary: Northern Trust is the cleanest custody-bank entry in today's broad financials breakout — VCP, RS new high, RSI 68, EPS +43%. The financials group is moving together which is confirmation, not coincidence.
analyses.full_analysis: The financials group breakout today is one of the strongest thematic moves in the scan: NTRS, BK, GS, MS, CFG, AFL, AIZ, C are all at or near 52-week highs simultaneously. This kind of group confirmation — where the RS lines of 8+ names in a sector all rise together — is one of the highest-conviction signals in market analysis. NTRS is the pick within the group because it has the cleanest RSI (68 — not overbought), a proper VCP (3 contractions, 3.6% tightest), and EPS growing +43%. Northern Trust manages $1.4T in assets and benefits directly from rising asset prices and increasing wealth management demand. Earnings are expected mid-July.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +43%, AUM growing with market. Custody banking has enormous switching costs — once a pension fund is with Northern Trust, they don't leave. Fee revenue grows with AUM.

technical.summary: VCP 3c tightest 3.6%, RSI 68, RS new high, at 52w high. TV BUY (14/3). Group confirmation from 8+ financial names all breaking out together.

earnings.summary: Expected mid-July. Consistent beat history. Rising markets = rising fee revenue, making the next report likely strong.

analyst.summary: Buy-tilted consensus; targets $190-210. Financials sector upgrades accelerating.

valuation.summary: ~14x forward PE — reasonable for a custody bank with NTRS's growth and moat.

risk.summary: R:R 2.3:1 ($162 stop, $211 target). Rate sensitivity is the main risk — any hawkish Fed pivot would hurt the group. Watch 10-year yields.

catalyst.summary: Wealth management AUM growth, ETF market expansion (custody fees), bank earnings season mid-July. Group breakout itself is a momentum catalyst.

analyses.summary: Goldman Sachs at an all-time high with M&A reaccelerating — EPS +24%, RS new high, RSI 64. Earnings July 14 create a 26-day pre-report window. Widely held in user portfolios, rewarding holders.
analyses.full_analysis: Goldman is the purest capital-markets and M&A play in the financial sector. When deal volumes pick up — as they are now — GS revenues surge because advisory fees are high-margin. The stock is at an all-time high with RSI 64 (not overbought), RS at a new high, and EPS growing +24%. Earnings are July 14, meaning the next 26 days should be a pre-earnings drift window as buy-side investors position for what looks like a strong quarter. Multiple users hold GS at cost bases ranging from $140 (massive gain) to $800 (underwater). Users at $140 should trim some to lock in the gain but keep a core. Users at $800 should hold — GS is fundamentally strong and the gap down from $800 to $1,100+ is still a meaningful loss to ride out, not a reason to sell now.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +24%, revenue +15%. M&A advisory, trading, and asset management all firing. Investment banking pipeline is reported to be the strongest since 2021.

technical.summary: At ATH, RS new high, RSI 64. TV BUY (14/2). Clean breakout confirmed by multiple dimensions.

earnings.summary: Earnings July 14 — 26-day pre-window. Prior quarter was a beat-and-raise. M&A rebound likely drives another strong report.

analyst.summary: Buy consensus; targets $1,100-1,350. JPMorgan reiterated Overweight on M&A cycle upgrade.

valuation.summary: ~13x forward PE — reasonable for GS's franchise. Capital markets firms trade on cycle, not just PE.

insider.summary: Modest insider selling at ATH is typical for large-cap financials executives; nothing unusual.
signals: Normal executive stock sales at ATH

risk.summary: R:R 2.5:1 ($1,028 stop, $1,341 target). M&A volumes can dry up if macro deteriorates. Earnings July 14 is a binary event to size around — trim 20% before report.

catalyst.summary: Earnings July 14 is the immediate catalyst. M&A pipeline, IPO calendar (SPCX itself is a sign of the market), and wealth management AUM growth are the sustained drivers.

analyses.summary: Caterpillar at an all-time high is the industrial bellwether confirming the entire economy is healthy — RS new high, RSI 68, VCP 6 contractions, EPS +30%. When CAT is at ATH with a tight base, the bull market is on solid footing.
analyses.full_analysis: Caterpillar builds the construction and mining equipment that moves civilization. When CAT is at an all-time high, it means construction, infrastructure, and mining are all expanding simultaneously — that's a broad economic statement. Today the stock has a 6-contraction VCP with tightest 9.0% (wide for a VCP but normal given $991 price), RS at new highs, RSI 68, EPS +30%. TV has 16 buy signals and 0 sells. The $1,000 round number just absorbed its resistance role and is now becoming support. Earnings July 22 create a 34-day pre-report window. The key risk is cyclicality — if macro data deteriorates suddenly, CAT is among the first names to sell off.

fundamentals.summary: EPS +30%, revenue +22%. Infrastructure spending (IIJA, CHIPS Act) sustaining demand. Mining equipment demand elevated on copper/lithium mining for energy transition.
signals: Mining demand for energy transition metals

technical.summary: VCP 6c tightest 9.0%, RSI 68, RS new high, at ATH. TV STRONG BUY (16/0). The $1,000 level has been absorbed.

earnings.summary: Earnings July 22 — 34-day pre-window. Consistent beat history when construction/mining cycles are running.

analyst.summary: Buy consensus with targets $1,050-1,200. Infrastructure spending cycle supports multi-year thesis.

valuation.summary: ~20x forward PE — fair for CAT at ATH in an infrastructure cycle. Not cheap but not bubble territory.

risk.summary: R:R 2.5:1 ($912 stop, $1,189 target). Cyclicality is the main risk — economic slowdown data could hit hard. Earnings July 22.

catalyst.summary: Infrastructure spending (IIJA implementation), copper/lithium mining expansion, data center construction equipment (ASML's fab construction uses CAT). Earnings July 22.

analyses.summary: SpaceX — NEW LISTING (June 2026, 5 days of price history). This is a thesis call on the world's most important private-turned-public space company. Technically unreadable but fundamentally extraordinary. Size very small; this is a 1-2% position concept, not a full bet.
analyses.full_analysis: SpaceX began trading on Nasdaq in June 2026 with a $2.4T market cap — the largest US IPO in history. The company has two primary businesses: (1) Starlink, a satellite internet constellation with 5M+ subscribers growing 50%+ YoY, each paying ~$120/month in subscription revenue — this is a SaaS business at scale in space; (2) Launch services, where Falcon 9's reusability gives SpaceX a 40-60% cost advantage over competitors, capturing ~70% of global commercial launches. The third optionality is Starship, which if successful could transform both Mars exploration and point-to-point Earth transport. The valuation ($2.4T) prices in 20+ years of extraordinary execution with no margin for error — that's the dominant risk. User RWaiB5nNl5TjiWamTOEMb8tgVgQ2 holds 5 shares at $160.95 avg cost ($181.65 current) — 12.7% gain in 5 days. Advice: hold with a wide stop, do not add aggressively, let the stock establish its trading range over the next 3-6 months before building a real position.

fundamentals.summary: Starlink has 5M+ subscribers at ~$120/month = ~$7.2B annualized run-rate revenue, growing 50%+ YoY. Launch services are profitable with Falcon 9 reusability. Financials as a public company are preliminary — treat numbers as approximate.
signals: Launch margins improving via Falcon 9 reusability

technical.summary: Only 5 days of trading history — no MA50, no 52-week reference, no VCP, no RS signal. Price is $181.65, down 14% from the opening-day high of $211. Expect 10-20% daily swings as the float settles.

earnings.summary: No public earnings history yet as a listed company. First quarterly report expected Q3 2026.
signals: No track record as a public company

analyst.summary: Coverage just initiating. Early targets range from $150 (bears citing valuation) to $280 (bulls citing Starlink optionality). Wide dispersion reflects uncertainty.

valuation.summary: $2.4T market cap on a company with negative public EPS and 5 days of history. The forward PE is literally negative (-2018x on available data). This is a pure growth/story multiple. The valuation is extreme by any traditional measure.

insider.summary: Lock-up expiry is the dominant insider dynamic — early employees and investors have massive paper gains and will sell on schedule. Watch for lock-up date announcements (typically 6 months post-IPO).

risk.summary: Highest-risk name in the scan. Primary risks: extreme valuation, no trading history, lock-up overhang, Elon Musk key-man risk (distraction from Tesla/X), and potential regulatory headwinds (FCC spectrum). Size at 1-2% max, not 5-10%.

catalyst.summary: Starlink subscriber milestones (10M would be transformational), Starship orbital flights confirming reusability, institutional inclusion (S&P 500 eligibility after 12 months), and first public earnings. These are 6-18 month catalysts, not today.
